title: mentha × niliaca var. genevensis
intro: Mentha × niliaca var. genevensis, also known as Geneva peppermint, is a hybrid of peppermint (Mentha × piperita) and watermint (Mentha aquatica). It is a perennial herb that has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The health benefits of Geneva peppermint include its ability to relieve stress and anxiety, improve respiratory function, and even help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Geneva peppermint can be prepared through various herbal methods, including teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
